question: Who is the Chief Guest at India's 77th Republic Day celebrations?
options: Ursula von der Leyen & Antonio Costa
detail:
- Two Top Leaders of the European Union (EU) are the Chief Guests for India's 77th Republic Day.
  - **Ursula von der Leyen** - President, European Commission.
  - **Antonio Costa** - President, European Council.

**Ursula von der Leyen**
- She is a German politician.
- She became the President of the European Commission for the first time on December 1, 2019.
- In July 2024, she was elected for a Second Term.
- She is the First Woman to reach this position (First Woman President).
- She has served as Defence Minister (2013–2019) in the government of German Chancellor Angela Merkel.

**European Commission**
- It is the Executive Body of the European Union (EU), which proposes EU laws, implements policies, and manages the administration of the entire EU.
- **Establishment:** 1958
- **Headquarters:** Brussels, Belgium

**Antonio Costa**
- He is a Portuguese Politician.
- Currently, he is the President, European Council.
- He assumed the office of President of the European Council on December 1, 2024.
- He has previously served as the Prime Minister of Portugal (2015–2024).

**European Council**
- It is the Supreme Political Body of the European Union (EU), which decides the Long-Term Policy and Strategic Decisions of the EU.
- **Establishment:** 1974
- **Headquarters:** Brussels, Belgium

**Note**
- **First Chief Guest:** President Sukarno of Indonesia (1950).
- **76 th Republic Day Chief Guest:** President Prabowo Subianto of Indonesia.

question: Which astronaut was awarded the country's highest peacetime gallantry award 'Ashok Chakra' on Republic Day 2026?
detail:
- President Droupadi Murmu awarded the Ashok Chakra to IAF officer turned astronaut Group Captain **Shubhanshu Shukla**.
- He was the first Indian to go to the ISS (International Space Station) and was part of Axiom Mission-4 (June 2025).
- He was the pilot of the SpaceX Dragon spacecraft.
- Shubhanshu Shukla became the Second Indian astronaut to receive the Ashok Chakra; before him, Rakesh Sharma (1984) received this honor.

**Ashok Chakra**
- It is India's Highest Peacetime Gallantry Award.
- It was established on 4 January 1952.

**Shubhanshu Shukla**
- **Born:** Lucknow, Uttar Pradesh.
- **ISS Mission:** Axiom-4 (June 2025).

question: Who has been appointed as the new President of the Olympic Council of Asia (OCA)?
options: Sheikh Joaan bin Hamad Al Thani
detail:
- Qatar Olympic Committee President Sheikh Joaan bin Hamad Al Thani has been elected as the President of the Olympic Council of Asia.
- Indian Sports Administrator Randhir Singh has resigned from the post of President of the Olympic Council of Asia (OCA).
- Raja Randhir Singh was elected unopposed as the President of the Olympic Council of Asia (OCA) at the 44th General Assembly of the OCA in New Delhi in September 2024.
- Olympic shooter Raja Randhir Singh was the First Indian to become the President of the Olympic Council of Asia (OCA).
- His tenure was for 2024–2028 (4 years).

**Olympic Council of Asia (OCA)**
- It is a governing body for sports in Asia.
- It is an organization that promotes Olympic games and sports activities in Asia.
- **Establishment:** 1982
- **Headquarters:** Kuwait City, Kuwait

question: Ajit Pawar, who recently passed away in a plane crash, was the Deputy Chief Minister of which state?
detail:
- Maharashtra Politician Ajit Pawar passed away at the age of 66 in a plane crash; he was the Deputy Chief Minister of Maharashtra.
- On 28 January 2026, a Learjet 45 Aircraft crashed during landing at Baramati Airport in Pune District.
- In the accident, a total of 5 people died, including Maharashtra Deputy CM Ajit Pawar (66 Years).
- He was going to Baramati for the Zilla Parishad Elections Campaign.
- CM Devendra Fadnavis announced a 3-day state mourning.

**Ajit Pawar – Nationalist Congress Party (Ajit Pawar)**
- He has been the Deputy CM of Maharashtra a record 6 times.
- Elected continuously for 7 times from Baramati Assembly seat.
- **First MP:** 1991 (Baramati).

question: What is India's rank in the Brand Finance Global Soft Power Index 2026?

**What is Global Soft Power Index?**
- The Global Soft Power Index is an Annual Report.
- It shows which countries of the world are most Powerful in terms of "Image + Effects".
- Brand Finance released the Global Soft Power Index 2026.
- In the Index, 193 UN Member Countries were ranked based on 55 Indicators.

| 2nd | China | 73.5 | +0.7 |

**Brand Finance**
- Brand Finance is the world's Leading Brand Valuation Consultancy.
- **Establishment:** 1996
- **Headquarters:** London, United Kingdom

question: President Droupadi Murmu has addressed the joint sitting of Parliament at the beginning of the Budget Session. In which article does the President have the right to address the joint sitting of Parliament?
detail:
- President Droupadi Murmu addressed the Joint Session of Parliament on the first day of Budget Session 2026.

**Article 87 – Special Address of the President**
- Gives the President the right to address the Joint Sitting of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha at the beginning of the first session after each general election and at the beginning of the first session of every year (Budget Session).
- In which the government's Policies and Future Plans are stated.

question: Arijit Singh has announced his retirement from playback singing, he has been awarded the Padma Shri in which year?
detail:

- **Filmfare Awards:** Won the title of Best Playback Singer a total of 8 times.

question: The United Nations has declared 2026 as which International Year?
options: International Year of Pastures and Pastoralists
International Year of Quantum Science and Technology
detail:
- The year 2026 has been declared as the **International Year for Rangelands and Pastoralists**.
- To raise awareness among people about the conservation of pastures, livelihoods of pastoralists, and the importance of sustainable animal husbandry.
- According to Scientists, grasslands are capable of absorbing carbon and play an important role in Biodiversity Conservation and Climate Balance.
- **Note:** Before this, the United Nations had declared 2026 as the International Year of the Woman Farmer.
- 2029 has been declared by the UN as the International Year of Asteroid Awareness and Planetary Defence.

question: Which two wetlands have been recognized as new Ramsar Sites?
detail:
- Union Environment Minister Bhupender Yadav has announced the inclusion of 2 new wetlands in the Ramsar List.

**Patna Bird Sanctuary**
- Patna Bird Sanctuary located in Etah, UP has been given the status of Ramsar Site.
- It has become the 11th Ramsar Wetland of Uttar Pradesh.
- 178 Bird Species and 252 Plant Species have been recorded here — high biodiversity zone.
- Birdlife International has also declared it as IBA (Important Bird Area).

**Chhari-Dhand Wetland**
- Chhari-Dhand Wetland located in Kutch, Gujarat was also included in the Ramsar list.
- It is situated near the Banni Grasslands.
- About 30,000 Common Cranes visit here every year.
- Here, along with migratory birds, species like Chinkara, Wolf, Caracal, Desert Fox are found.

**Ramsar Sites in India**
- India signed this treaty on 1 February 1982.
- First Sites: Chilika Lake (Odisha) and Keoladeo National Park (Rajasthan).
- Most Ramsar Sites are in Tamil Nadu.
- **Note:** Now the number of Ramsar Sites in India has increased from 96 to **98**.

question: Who has won the men's singles title of the Australian Open 2026?

- He has become the youngest player to complete a Career Grand Slam.

**Australian Open**
- Australian Open is a Prestigious Tournament of tennis.
- Australian Open started in 1905 as "Australian Championships".
- It is played on Synthetic Hard Court.

question: Which country has become the 21st member of the Eurozone from 1 January, 2026?
detail:
- Bulgaria has become the 21st Member of the Eurozone from 1 January, 2026.
- Now the Official Currency of Bulgaria has become Euro.

**What is Eurozone?**
- This is the Group of European Union countries where the Official Currency is Euro.
- European Union has 27 Members but Eurozone has only 21 Members.
- **Formation:** 1 January 1999

**European Union**
- It is a union of 27 countries of Europe.
- EU has about 5.5% of the world's Population but it contributes about 14 to 17% to the world's GDP.
- **Formation:** 1993 (Created by Treaty Of Maastricht)
- **Largest country of EU:** Germany
- **Newest member:** Croatia (1 July 2013)
- **Note:** In 2020, UK left the European Union, after which it had 27 Members.

question: Which country has granted legal rights to stingless bees found in the Amazon rainforest?
detail:
- Peru has granted legal rights to stingless bees found in the Amazon rainforests.
- They have become the first insects in the world to receive legal protection similar to humans.
- Found in the Peruvian part of the Amazon rainforest.

**These bees have now received the following inherent rights**
- Right to existence.
- Right to maintain a healthy population.
- Right to a pollution-free, healthy habitat.
- Right to flourish in the natural environment.

**Stingless bee (Meliponini)**
- These bees are one of the oldest Species on Earth. They are responsible for Pollination of more than 80 percent of trees and plants in the Amazon.
- The world's oldest bee species, which has existed for about 80 million years.
- They have stings, but they are very small.
- Globally, about 500 species exist, out of which more than 170 species are found specifically in Peru.

question: Who has been appointed as the head coach of the Indian women's hockey team?
detail:
- Sjoerd Marijne has been appointed as the new Head Coach of the Indian Women's Hockey Team; before this, the head coach of the team was Harendra Singh.
- Sjoerd Marijne is a Dutch (Netherlands) hockey coach.
- He handled the work of Head Coach of Indian Women's Hockey Team for the first time in 2017.
- In Tokyo Olympics 2020, the Indian Women's Hockey Team played Semifinals for the first time in history and achieved the fourth position.
- In August 2021, he Resigned from the post.

**Indian Women's Hockey Team**
- **Captain:** Salima Tete (Jharkhand)
- **World Ranking:** 5
- **Olympic Best:** 4th (1980 & 2020)

question: The Jallikattu festival 2026 was held in which state of India?
detail:
- Jallikattu festival was organized in Thachankurichi village, Pudukkottai district in Tamil Nadu state.
- It is organized on the day of Mattu Pongal of Pongal festival — it is celebrated in January.

**Jallikattu Festival**
- Jallikattu is more than 2000 years old and is part of ancient Tamil culture.
- Jallikattu is a Traditional Game of Tamil Nadu.
- In this, a game of controlling bulls is played by the communities there.
- The name of the game comes from "Salli" (Coins) + "Kattu" (Tied).

**Constitutional and Legal Aspects**
- In 2014, the Supreme Court banned Jallikattu.
- This sport has been permitted under the Tamil Nadu Animal Protection Act, 2017.
- In 2023, the Supreme Court upheld the validity of the Tamil Nadu Jallikattu law.

question: According to the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), which state of India recorded the highest number of tiger deaths in the year 2025?
detail:
- According to the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA), 166 Tiger deaths were recorded in the year 2025.
- The highest Tiger Deaths were recorded in Madhya Pradesh (55).
- About 75% of the world's Tiger Population is found in India.

**Most Tiger Deaths (State-wise – 2025)**
- Madhya Pradesh - 55 (Tiger State of India)
- Maharashtra - 38
- Kerala - 13

**National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA)**
- It is an Agency for Wildlife Conservation.
- It was formed under Section 38 L (1) of Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972.
- **Aim:** To Conserve Bengal Tiger.
- **Work:** To oversee Management of Project Tiger and Tiger Reserves in India.
- **Formed:** December 2005
- **Ministry:** Ministry Of Environment, Forest And Climate Change

question: The book 'Agni Sarassulo Vikasinchina Kamalam' is based on whose life?
detail:
- A Telugu Book based on the life of India's President Droupadi Murmu was released in Visakhapatnam (Andhra University), Andhra Pradesh.
- **Book:** 'Agni Sarassulo Vikasinchina Kamalam Droupadi Murmu'.
- **Meaning:** "The Lotus that Bloomed in the Pool of Fire: Droupadi Murmu".
- This book depicts the life of India's President Droupadi Murmu from Tribal Roots to Rashtrapati Bhavan.
- The author of this book is Yarlagadda Lakshmi Prasad, who is a Former MP and President of Vishwa Hindi Parishad.

**President Droupadi Murmu**
- 15th President of India.
- **Born:** 20 June 1958 (Mayurbhanj, Odisha)
- India's first Tribal President.

question: Who has set a record for winning the award at the youngest age at the 83rd Golden Globe Awards 2026?
detail:

- It is one of the world's most prestigious cinema awards.
- **Owen Cooper** (UK, 16 years) has become the youngest winner to win Golden Globe Awards.
- He was given the Best supporting actor award (Tv Series - Adolescence).

| Best Motion Picture – Musical or Comedy | One Battle After Another |
| Best Motion Picture – Non-English Language | The Secret Agent |

- It is a prestigious American Film and Television Awards Ceremony, which is organized annually by Hollywood Foreign Press Association (HFPA).
- These awards are given especially to honor the best works of Movies & Television Shows.
- **Establishment:** Golden Globes was organized for the first time in 1944.

question: Shaksgam Valley is situated in which State/Union Territory of India?
detail:
- Expansion of China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) has been announced by China and Pakistan.
- India's ministry of external affairs said that the 1963 China–Pakistan Boundary Agreement is completely illegal and invalid.
- India also rejected CPEC (China–Pakistan Economic Corridor) because it passes through Indian territory like PoK and Shaksgam Valley.
- Pakistan had illegally handed over 5,180 sq. km Indian territory (part of PoK) to China in 1963.

**Shaksgam Valley (Trans-Karakoram Tract)**
- **Location:** North of Ladakh region.
- Under China's control since 1963.

question: Which Indian Army officer has received the UN Secretary General's Award 2025 in January 2026?
detail:
- Indian Army officer Major Swathi Shantha Kumar has received the UN's prestigious 'Secretary-General’s Award 2025'.
- Currently, she is deployed under UN Peacekeeping Mission (UNMISS) in South Sudan.
- UNMISS stands for United Nations Mission in South Sudan, which is a UN peacekeeping mission run to maintain peace and protect civilians in South Sudan.
- She is leading the Indian Engagement Team in UNMISS (United Nations Mission in South Sudan).

**Major Swathi Shantha Kumar**
- She is from Bengaluru (Karnataka).
- Officer of the Corps of Electronics and Mechanical Engineers (EME) of the Indian Army.

**UN Secretary-General’s Award**
- Given to outstanding works promoting Gender Equality and Gender-Responsive Peacekeeping in UN Peacekeeping Missions.

question: According to the Ministry of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry and Dairying, what is the current position of India in fish production in the world?

- India produced Record 195 Million Tons fish production in 2024-25.
- India contributes about 8% to Global Fish Production.
- This sector gives employment to about Three crore fishermen and Fish Keepers.

question: India has submitted nominations for which heritage site to be included in the UNESCO World Heritage List for 2026–27?
detail:
- India has submitted the official nomination to UNESCO to include Meghalaya's famous **Living Root Bridges (Jingkieng Jri)** in the 2026–27 UNESCO World Heritage List.
- These bridges have been created by the **Khasi and Jaintia tribes** over centuries by connecting tree roots; it is an example of a **Living Cultural Landscape**.
- This landscape demonstrates a deep relationship between people, nature, and spirituality.

**UNESCO Sites in India**
- There are a total of **44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites** in India.
- These include 36 Cultural, 7 Natural, and 1 Mixed site.

question: The Supreme Court has extended the tenure till 31 July 2026 of which committee monitoring relief & rehabilitation of Manipur violence victims?
detail:

- The Committee has so far submitted **42 Reports** to the Supreme Court on Relief, Compensation, and Rehabilitation.

**Justice Geeta Mittal Committee**
- This committee was constituted on **7 August 2023**.
- **Chairman:** Former Chief Justice of Jammu and Kashmir High Court, **Gita Mittal**.
- **Members:** Shalini P. Joshi, Asha Menon.

**Note**
- In August 2025, Parliament extended the duration of President's Rule in Manipur for 6 months; Manipur CM Biren Singh resigned from his post on 9 February.

question: Which state has been re-included in the 'Tiger State' list after 33 years?
detail:
- The **National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA)** has given **Gujarat** the status of Tiger State again after 33 years.
- The presence of 1 Tiger has been confirmed in **Ratanmahal Wildlife Sanctuary** of Gujarat, and due to this, Gujarat has been given the status of Tiger State.

**What is Tiger State?**
- States where Tigers reside are given the status of Tiger State by the National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA).

**Why Was Gujarat Excluded From The Tiger State?**
- In the 1989 Tiger Census in Gujarat, tiger marks were found but no tiger was seen.
- For this reason, the status of Tiger State was taken away from Gujarat in **1992**.

**Ratanmahal Wildlife Sanctuary**
- Also known as **Ratanmahal Sloth Bear Sanctuary**.
- **Location:** Dahod district, Gujarat.
- **Area:** 55.65 Sq. km.
- **Established:** 1982.

question: Biswa Bandhu Sen passed away in January 2026. He was the Speaker of the Legislative Assembly of which state?
detail:

- Biswa Bandhu Sen was an Indian politician.

**Biswa Bandhu Sen**
- Belongs to **Tripura**.
- **Deputy Speaker of the Tripura Legislative Assembly:** 21 June 2018 – 2 March 2023.
- **11th Speaker of the Tripura Legislative Assembly:** 24 March 2023 – 26 December 2025.

question: How many children have been given the Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar (PMRBP) 2025 in total?
detail:
- **Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar (PMRBP) 2025** has been given to a total of **20 children**.
- These awards are given to children younger than **18 years** in **6 categories**.
- This time, this award was given to 2 children **Posthumously**.

**Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar**
- It is a National Level Award.
- It is given every year by the **President**.
- Maximum 25 awards are given every year.
- It is given to children aged 5 to 18 years for outstanding achievements.
- **Categories (6):** Bravery, Social Service, Environment, Sports, Art & Culture, and Science & Technology.
- **Ministry:** Ministry of Women and Child Development.

question: Which country has become the first to recognize Somaliland as an independent state?
detail:
- **Israel** has become the first country to Officially declare **Somaliland** as an Independent and Sovereign country.
- Along with this, Israel has also signed an Agreement to establish Diplomatic Relations.

**What Is Somaliland?**
- It is a Region inside the country of **Somalia**.
- It has its own Government, Police, Currency, and Passport.
- There is Democracy and peace in this Region, while Civil War has been going on in Somalia for years.
- The country of Somalia does not recognize it.

**Why And How Did It Come To Be Different?**
- Before 1960, Somalia was in two parts.
- **North Somalia** was under British Control and was called **British Somaliland**.
- The **South** part was under Italian Control and was called **Italian Somaliland**.
- Both became independent in 1960 and together formed the "Somali Republic" (Somalia).
- After 10 years of War, the people of Somaliland separated themselves from Somalia in 1991 and formed a new Somaliland country.

- This is Carlsen's **6th World Rapid title**.
- With this, he won a total of **19th World Championship Title** of his career.
- In the Women’s Category, Russia's **Aleksandra Goryachkina** became the winner.
- **Arjun Erigaisi** won the Bronze Medal in Men's Categories.
- **Koneru Humpy** won the Bronze Medal in Women's Categories.

**Men’s Section (Open Category)**

| Position | Player Name | Country | Medal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First | Magnus Carlsen | Norway | Gold |
| Second | Vladislav Artemiev | Russia | Silver |
| Third | Arjun Erigaisi | India | Bronze |

**Women’s Section**

| Position | Player Name | Country | Medal |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First | Aleksandra Goryachkina | Russia | Gold |
| Second | Zhu Jiner | China | Silver |
| Third | Koneru Humpy | India | Bronze |

**International Chess Federation (FIDE)**
- It is an international governing body for Chess.
- **Formation:** July 20, 1924.
- **Headquarters:** Lausanne, Switzerland.
- **President:** Arkady Dvorkovich (Russian).
- **Deputy President:** Viswanathan Anand (India).

question: Khaleda Zia passed away in January 2026. She was the first female Prime Minister of which country?
detail:
- **Khaleda Zia** passed away at the age of 80.
- She has been the **First female Prime Minister of Bangladesh**.
- She was the Chairperson of the Bangladesh Nationalist Party (BNP).
- She became the PM of Bangladesh **3 times**.
  - **1st Tenure:** 1991 to 1996 (Start of parliamentary system of government).
  - **2nd Tenure:** Feb 1996 (Brief Tenure).
  - **3rd Tenure:** 2001 to 2006.

question: The Parvati-Arga Bird Sanctuary has been declared an Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ). This bird sanctuary belongs to which state?
detail:
- The Indian Government declared **Parvati- Arga Bird Sanctuary (PABS)** located in **Uttar Pradesh** as an Eco-Sensitive Zone (ESZ).
- This announcement was made under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC).
- Its objective is to strengthen Biodiversity Conservation in Parvati-Arga Bird Sanctuary (PABS) and promote Responsible Ecotourism.

**What is ESZ (Eco-Sensitive Zone)?**
- ESZ (Eco-Sensitive Zone) are areas that are buffer zones created around Protected Areas (like National Parks and Wildlife Sanctuaries), whose objective is to regulate human activities and ensure sustainable development.
- Eco-Sensitive Zones are declared under the **Environment (Protection) Act, 1986**.

**Parvati-Arga Bird Sanctuary**
- **Location:** Uttar Pradesh (Gonda District, UP).
- **Area:** 1,084 hectares (11 sq km).
- **Ramsar Site:** 2019.
- Parvati-Arga Bird Sanctuary is an important habitat for migratory birds coming from Central Asia and Tibet.

question: President Droupadi Murmu sailed in the indigenous submarine INS Vaghsheer. INS Vaghsheer is a submarine of which class?
detail:
- **Droupadi Murmu** sailed in the Indian Navy's indigenous submarine **INS Vaghsheer**.
- She has become the **Second President** to sail in a submarine after Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam.
- This journey was made from **Karwar Naval Base** (Western Coast) (Karnataka).
- Navy Chief Admiral Dinesh K. Tripathi was also present with the President.
- Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam had made a sea voyage (sortie) on the Indian Navy submarine INS Sindhurakshak on 13 February 2006.

**INS Vaghsheer**
- **Project:** Project-75 (Scorpene Project).
- **Class:** Kalvari Class.
- **Type:** Diesel–Electric Conventional Submarine.
- **Position:** 6th & Last Submarine of Kalvari Class.
- **Commission:** 23rd January 2024.
- **Surface speed:** Approx 11 knots/h.
- **Submerged speed:** Approx 20 knots/h.
- **Other Submarines:** Kalvari, Khanderi, Karanj, Vela, Vagir.
- **Built By:** Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL).

**What is Project-75 (Scorpene Project)?**
- Indian Navy's Submarine construction program.
- Scorpene-class Submarines with the help of **France**.
- **Objective:** To strengthen Underwater Warfare Capability.
- **Note:** 'Vaghsheer' is named after a predatory fish 'Sand Fish' of the Indian Ocean.

question: In 2025, what rank did India secure in Rare Earth Oxide (REO) reserves?
detail:
- **India** secured **3rd Rank** in Rare Earth Reserves in the **Amicus Growth Report**.
- The Amicus Growth Report has been released by Amicus Growth Advisors.
- India has **6.9 Million Tonnes (MT)** Rare Earth Oxide (REO) Reserves.
- This is about 6–7% of Global Reserves.

**Rare Earth Elements**
- A group of 17 special metals found in the earth's internal structures.
- They are used in the manufacture of Modern technology, green energy, and defense equipment.

**Top REE Reserves (Top 3)**
- 1. **1st:** China – 44 MT 2. **2nd:** Brazil – ~21 MT 3. **3rd:** India – 6.9 MT

**REE Production (2024)**
- **China:** 2,70,000 tonnes (1st)
- **USA:** 45,000 tonnes (2nd)
- **Myanmar:** 31,000 tonnes (3rd)
- **India:** 2,900 tonnes (7th) - Less than 1 percent.

**Amicus Growth Advisors**
- Amicus Growth Advisors is a Consulting organization that advises companies and the government on Business Growth, Policy Research, and Market Analysis.
- **Headquarters:** New Delhi.
- **Union Minister of Coal and Mines:** G Kishan Reddy.

question: In which country is Mukalla Port located?
detail:
- **Saudi Arabia** has carried out Airstrikes on **Mukalla Port** (Hadramout Province) of **Yemen**.
- This attack by Saudi was carried out targeting the Arms Shipment sent for the UAE-backed separatist group (**Southern Transitional Council - STC**).
- According to Saudi Arabia, weapons were being transported by ships from Fujairah Port of UAE to Mukalla Port (Yemen).

question: In which state has the 'Green to Gold' initiative been launched to promote cannabis cultivation?
detail:
- **Himachal Pradesh** CM **Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu** has launched the ‘Green To Gold’ Initiative.
- The objective of this scheme is to increase farmers' income and strengthen the state's Economy by promoting the cultivation of **Industrial Hemp**.
- Under this initiative, cannabis, which is considered a wild weed, will be turned into a valuable Economic Resource.
- **Goal:** To make Himachal a self-reliant state in agriculture by 2027 and develop Industrial Hemp as an Income-generating Crop.

**Uses of Hemp**
- Pharmaceuticals, Textiles, Paper, Cosmetics, Biofuel, Eco-friendly Construction.

question: Who has become the second Indian player to complete 10,000 runs in women's international cricket?
detail:
- **Smriti Mandhana** became the second Indian and fourth female player overall to complete 10,000 runs in women's international cricket.
- Apart from Smriti Mandhana, Mithali Raj, Suzie Bates (New Zealand), and Charlotte Edwards (England) have also completed 10,000 runs in international cricket.
- Smriti Mandhana is now tied with **Meg Lanning (Australia)** for the most international centuries (17) in women's cricket.

**Smriti Mandhana**
- Belongs to **Mumbai**.
- **Arjuna Award:** 2019.
- **Wisden Leading Cricketer in the World:** 2025.
- **ICC ODI Cricketer of the Year:** 2024.
- **ICC Cricketer of the Year:** 2018 and 2021.

question: Mamady Doumbouya has been elected as the President of which country?
detail:
- Some time ago, Presidential Elections were held in **Guinea**.
- Following which **Mamady Doumbouya** has become the new President of Guinea.
- He secured **86.72% votes** in the Presidential Election.
- Doumbouya had ousted the then President **Alpha Condé** from power in 2021.
- His victory marks the return of **Civilian Rule** in Guinea.

**Guinea**
- It is a country in West Africa.
- **Capital:** Conakry.
- **Prime Minister:** Bah Oury.
- Guinea has the world's **Largest Bauxite Reserve**.

question: Which foundation day of DRDO was celebrated on 1 January 2026?
detail:
- **Defence Research And Development Organisation (DRDO)** celebrated its **68th Foundation Day** on 01 January 2026.

**DRDO**
- It is a premier organization of the Indian Government, which develops Defence Technologies And Systems for the Indian Defence Forces.
- **Headquarters:** New Delhi.
- It works under the **Indian Ministry Of Defence**.
- **Chairman:** Dr. Sameer V Kamat (26 August, 2022).
- The Central Government has given him a one-year service extension to continue as the head of DRDO till 31 May 2025.

question: The proposal to declare Kaimur Wildlife Sanctuary as a tiger reserve has been approved, it belongs to which state?
detail:
- **Kaimur Wildlife Sanctuary** is located in **Bihar**.
- The Bihar Government has approved the proposal to declare Kaimur Wildlife Sanctuary as a Tiger Reserve.
- This proposal will be sent to the **National Tiger Conservation Authority (NTCA)** for final approval.
- Kaimur Wildlife Sanctuary is the **largest Wildlife Sanctuary** in Bihar.
- Currently, Bihar's only Tiger Reserve is: **Valmiki Tiger Reserve**.
- It is located in the West Champaran district of Bihar.

**Kaimur Wildlife Sanctuary**
- **Establishment:** 1979.
- **Location:** Kaimur and Rohtas Districts, Bihar.
- **Area:** 1,505 Km2.

question: Who chaired the task force set up to improve India's sports governance?
detail:
- **Abhinav Bindra** chaired the Task Force constituted to improve **Sports Governance** in India.
- The Task Force constituted under the chairmanship of Olympic Medallist Abhinav Bindra submitted the report on India's Sports Governance.
- This Task Force was recently constituted by the Government of India (during 2025–26).
- The Report presented the **Systemic Weaknesses** of sports administration and a **Roadmap** for improvement.

**Objectives**
- To implement Good Governance Practices in **National Sports Federations (NSFs)**.
- To ensure Athlete Welfare & Protection.
- To make the Selection Process Fair & Transparent.
- To prevent Conflict of Interest.
- To align India with Global Sporting Standards.

**Abhinav Bindra**
- Former Indian sports shooter.
- Belongs to **Uttarakhand**.
- He is the first Indian to win an **Individual Olympic Gold Medal**.
- He won this Medal in the Men’s 10m Air Rifle Event at the **Beijing Olympics 2008**.

question: Who has taken over as the Interim President of Venezuela?
detail:
- The US has launched Military Operation **Absolute Resolve** against Venezuela.
- After which Venezuela's President **Nicolás Maduro** was arrested by the USA.
- Following this, the Supreme Court of Venezuela appointed **Delcy Rodriguez** as the **Interim President**.

**Delcy Rodriguez**
- She has been the Vice President of the country since 2018.
- Senior Leader of the United Socialist Party of Venezuela (PSUV).
- **Foreign Minister:** 2014–2018.
- Venezuela has the **Top 3 Oil Reserves** In The World.

question: Who has been appointed the new Director General of Police (DGP) of Maharashtra?
detail:
- IPS officer **Sadanand Vasant Date** has been appointed as the new **Director General of Police (DGP)** of Maharashtra.
- Before this, **Rashmi Shukla** was the first female DGP of Maharashtra, who retired on 31 December 2025.

**Sadanand Date**
- He is a **1990 Batch** IPS Officer.
- He has also previously served as the Director General of the **National Investigation Agency (NIA)**.

question: Greenland is part of which country?
detail:
- US President Donald Trump had given a statement regarding occupying Greenland.
- **Greenland**, which is an Island of North America, is part of **Denmark**.

**Why does the U.S. want to occupy Greenland?**
- World's largest island (21 lakh sq km).
- Greenland is so big that it becomes a Natural platform to monitor the entire Arctic region.
- It is like a **Forward Security Post** for America and NATO.

**Greenland has huge quantities of**
- **Lithium** → EV batteries.
- **Uranium** → Nuclear Energy.
- **Graphite & Niobium** → Missiles, AI chips, smartphones.

**Climate Change & Northern Sea Routes**
- Arctic ice is melting due to Global Warming.
- This is opening up new **Northern Sea Routes**, which:
  - Make Asia–Europe travel possible in less time and cost.
  - Also, new oil, gas, and mineral resources are becoming available.
- Whoever controls Greenland will have a hold on Future Arctic Trade Routes.

**Greenland = Autonomous Region**
- Greenland is part of Denmark.
- But it has got **Self-Government**.
- **Prime Minister:** Jens-Friedrich Nielsen.

**Denmark**
- **Capital:** Copenhagen.
- **Prime Minister:** Mette Frederiksen.

question: In which state is the Kalai-II hydroelectric project in question proposed?
detail:
- **Kalai-II Hydroelectric Project** is being built on the **Lohit River** in **Arunachal Pradesh**.
- The **Expert Appraisal Committee (EAC)** of the Environment Ministry has recommended granting Environmental Clearance to the 1,200 MW Kalai-II Hydroelectric Project.
- Environmental activists are opposing this project because this area is the local habitat of the **White-bellied Heron**.

**Kalai-II Hydroelectric Project**
- **Location:** Anjaw District, Arunachal Pradesh.
- **River:** Lohit River (tributary of Brahmaputra).
- **Capacity:** 1200 MW.
- **Development:** Tehri Hydro Development Corporation Limited (THDC).
- **Cost:** ₹14,176 crore.

**Kalai-I Hydroelectric Project**
- **Location:** Anjaw District, Arunachal Pradesh.
- **River:** Lohit River (tributary of Brahmaputra).
- **Capacity:** 600 MW.

question: Which country’s citizens are allowed to travel to India under the e-B-4 Visa that has been in the news?
detail:
- India has launched a new **Electronic Business Visa – e-B-4** for **Chinese citizens**.
- Its objective is to make trade between India–China Easy, systematic, and secure.

**What is an e-B-4 Visa?**
- **Full Name:** E-Production Investment Business Visa (e-B-4).
- **Start:** From **1 January 2026**.
- **Process:** It is **100% online**. No need to go to any Embassy.
- **Time:** Issued in 45 to 50 days after application.
- **Duration:** Permission to stay in India for a maximum of 6 months.

**Why Was This Visa Needed?**
- Many Indian factories have Chinese machines installed, which require engineers from there to operate or repair them.
- Under this visa, Chinese Experts will be able to come to India for machine installation and maintenance, Quality Check, Employee Training, and IT/ERP system setup.
- apart from this, this visa will also be available for work related to Plant Design and Senior Management Meetings.

question: Pravasi Bharatiya Divas is celebrated every year on 9 January in India, when was it started?
detail:
- Pravasi Bharatiya Divas is celebrated every year on **9 January**.
- This day is celebrated to commemorate the return of **Mahatma Gandhi** from South Africa to India on 9 January 1915.

**When did it start?**
- **Start:** Year **2003**.
- **Initiative:** Government of India (Ministry of External Affairs).

**Pravasi Bharatiya Divas Conference**
- **18th Pravasi Bharatiya Divas Convention** was held in Bhubaneswar, Odisha from 8 to 10 January 2025.

question: The “Ungal Kanavai Sollungal” programme has been launched by which state government?
detail:
- The **Tamil Nadu** Government has launched a new Participatory Governance Programme called **‘Ungal Kanavai Sollungal’** (Tell Us Your Dreams).
- This was announced by Tamil Nadu Chief Minister **M.K. Stalin**.
- This initiative will be implemented across the state for **30 days**.
- There are about **1.91 crore families** in the state; aspirations from all will be collected.
- **50,000 Volunteers** will distribute forms door-to-door.

**Participatory Governance**
- Involving citizens directly in **Policy Making & Development Planning**, so that schemes are based on ground-level needs.

question: What is the most prominent feature of the Indian Army’s modern warfare force ‘Bhairav’?
detail:
- The Indian Army has constituted the **‘Bhairav’ Modern Warfare Force** with **1 lakh+ drone operators**.
- This force has been developed as a **Drone- centric Modern Warfare Unit**.
- It will include more than 1 lakh soldiers / Drone Operators.
- ‘Bhairav’ will be one of the world's largest **Drone-enabled Combat Units**.
- This force is capable of **Real-time Surveillance**, **Precision Strike**, and **Intelligence Gathering**.
- The objective of its formation is to increase India's **Battlefield Dominance**.

**What is 'Bhairav' Force?**
- It is a **Next-generation Combat Unit**, where every soldier is trained in Advanced Drone Operations and Tech-based Battlefield Operations.

**Key facts**
- So far **15 Bhairav Battalions** have been formed.
- It is deployed on both borders (**LAC & LOC**).
- It will be increased to **25 Battalions**.

question: Veteran environmentalist Madhav Gadgil passed away recently. In which year was he awarded the Padma Bhushan?
detail:
- Senior Environmentalist, **Madhav Gadgil** passed away in Pune at the age of 83.
- He is called by the name **"Son of the Earth"**.
- Madhav Gadgil was the Chairman of the **Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel (WGEEP)**; this is what is called the **Gadgil Commission (2010–11)**.

**Gadgil Commission (WGEEP)**
- In 2010–11, Madhav Gadgil chaired the Western Ghats Ecology Expert Panel.
- This commission recommended declaring the entire Western Ghats as an ecologically **Sensitive area**.

**Awards**
- 1981 - Padma Shri
- **2006 - Padma Bhushan**
- 2024 - Champions of the Earth

question: Nagori Ashwagandha product has been awarded the GI Tag. It belongs to which state?
detail:
- **Nagauri Ashwagandha** of **Nagaur** district of **Rajasthan** was granted the GI Tag.
- It has become Rajasthan's **second GI-tagged Agricultural Product**.
- **First:** Sojat Mehendi.
- Nagauri Ashwagandha is known for its **medicinal properties**.

**Geographical Indications (GI) Tags**
- India enacted the **Geographical Indications of Goods (Registration and Protection) Act, 1999**.
- It was implemented from **15 September 2003**.
- The first GI tag in India was given in **2004– 2005**.
- **GI Tag Issuer:** It is given by the **Geographical Indications Registry** which is located in **Chennai**.
- It works under the **Ministry of Commerce & Industry**.

question: Who was appointed as the new Chairman of the Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB)?
detail:
- **A. K. Balasubramaniam** took charge as the **Chairman** of the Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB).
- He has replaced **Dr. D. K. Shukla** on this post.

**A. K. Balasubramaniam**
- He is a **Mechanical Engineer**, trained in **Nuclear Engineering**.
- Before this, he has been **Director (Technical)** in NPCIL (Nuclear Power Corporation of India Limited).

**Atomic Energy Regulatory Board (AERB)**
- **Established:** 1983.
- It regulates **Nuclear & Radiation Safety**.
- It has been established under the **Atomic Energy Act, 1962**.
- **Headquarters:** Mumbai, Maharashtra.
- **Under:** Department of Atomic Energy (DAE).

question: Which has become India's first "completely paperless" district court?
detail:
- **Chief Justice of India (CJI) Surya Kant** inaugurated India's first 'Fully digital and paperless' district court in **Wayanad (Kalpetta)**, Kerala.
- Now in this court, from filing cases to delivering verdicts, all work will be done on **Computers and tablets**.
- This is India's **First District Court** where **Artificial Intelligence (AI)** will be used to help judges.

question: Which mascot has been launched by UIDAI to simplify Aadhaar-related communication?
detail:
- The Unique Identification Authority of India (**UIDAI**) launched the Official Mascot of Aadhaar **‘Udai’**.
- The Aadhaar Mascot ‘Udai’ was launched by UIDAI Chairman **Neelkanth Mishra**.
- It is a type of **ChatBot**, which will give information related to Aadhaar.
- The objective of ‘Udai’ is to explain Aadhaar-related information in simple language and increase **trust and understanding** among people.

**‘Udai’ will work like a “Communication Partner” which will**
- Explain difficult Aadhaar services in **Easy Language**.
- Tell people how to make **Safe Use** of Aadhaar.
- Increase **Trust and Understanding** about Aadhaar among people.

question: Which district will become India's first Bio- Happy District?
detail:
- **Keyi Panyor** of **Arunachal Pradesh** will become India's first **Bio-Happy District**.

**What is Bio Happiness?**
- When people's food, health, and income are improved by keeping **Nature and Biodiversity** safe, the happiness derived from it is called **Bio-Happiness**.
- This term was given by Agricultural Scientist **M.S. Swaminathan**.
- The Bio-Happy District initiative is being implemented by the **M. S. Swaminathan Research Foundation** with the support of the District Administration.

**Why was Kayi Panyor chosen?**
- This area is full of **Nature and Biodiversity**. Dense forests, clean rivers, and tribal communities doing traditional farming live here.
- For this reason, this District was considered most suitable for implementing a scheme that improves people's lives while saving nature.

**How will this project work?**
- **Farming and Biodiversity:** Saving local traditional farming and crops.
- **Livelihood:** Increasing local people's income without harming the environment.

question: The Weimar Triangle is a regional cooperation grouping of which three countries?
detail:
- **India** has held talks at the official level with the **Weimar Triangle (France–Germany– Poland)** for the first time.
- These talks were held in **Paris**.
- Foreign Minister **S. Jaishankar** was present on behalf of India.
- Poland's Foreign Minister **Radoslaw Sikorski** said that he is satisfied that India has started cutting Russian oil imports.

**What is Weimar Triangle?**
- The Weimar Triangle is a **regional diplomatic grouping**, which includes **France, Germany, and Poland**.
- **Objective:** To strengthen **European security, defence, economy, and political coordination**.
- **Established:** 1991.
- **Place of Origin:** Weimar city, Germany.
- The Weimar Triangle is a trilateral forum of France, Germany, and Poland, founded in 1991 to promote European security and cooperation.

question: Where is the Aralam Wildlife Sanctuary, now declared as India’s first butterfly sanctuary, located?
detail:
- **Aralam Wildlife Sanctuary** is located in **Kerala** and will now be known as **Aralam Butterfly Sanctuary**.
- The decision to change its name was taken by the **Kerala State Wildlife Board**.

**Why was the name changed?**
- Out of **Total 327 Butterfly Species** in Kerala, **266 Species** are found here, i.e., about **81%**.
- Due to having so many Butterfly Species, it has now been declared a **Butterfly Sanctuary**.

**Aralam Butterfly Sanctuary**
- Located in the **Western Ghats** in Kerala.
- This sanctuary is in **Kannur** district.
- The **Cheenkanni River** flows through Aralam Butterfly Sanctuary.
- **Area:** 55 KM2.
- **Established:** 1984.

question: Recently, Alyssa Healy announced her retirement from international Cricket. She is the captain of which country?
detail:
- **Australian Women Cricket Team** Captain and wicketkeeper-batter **Alyssa Healy** has announced her **Retirement** from International Cricket.
- She will retire after the home series against **India** in **March 2026**.
- She has been a part of the **World Cup Winner Team 8 times**:
  - 6 T20 World Cups
  - 2 ODI World Cups
- She took over the captaincy in 2023 after **Meg Lanning's** retirement.
- Alyssa Healy received the **ICC Women’s T20I Cricketer of the Year** award in **2018 and 2019**.

question: Which Indian diplomat has been appointed as Chairperson of the UN Advisory Board on Disarmament Matters?
detail:
- **D.B. Venkatesh Varma** has been appointed as the **Chairman** of the **Advisory Board on Disarmament Matters** by the UN Secretary-General.
- D.B. Venkatesh Varma is the **First Indian** to become the chairman of this organization.
- **Tenure:** 2026-27.

**D.B. Venkatesh Varma**
- He is a **1988 batch IFS officer** (retired in 2021).
- He is a **Former Diplomat** of India.
- He has been India's Ambassador to **Russia**.
- He had an important role in the **India-US Civil Nuclear Deal** negotiations. He is considered an expert on **Disarmament**.

**UN Advisory Board on Disarmament Matters**
- It is a **UN advisory body** that gives **policy advice** to the UN Secretary-General on arms control, nuclear disarmament, and strategic stability.
- **Established:** 1978 (after the 10th Special Session of UNGA).
- **Headquarters:** New York, USA.

question: Who has been appointed as the new director general of Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP)?
detail:
- **Shatrujeet Singh Kapoor** has been appointed as the **Director General** of the **Indo-Tibetan Border Police (ITBP)**.
- He has replaced **Praveen Kumar**, who has become the new Director General of the **Border Security Force (BSF)**.
- Shatrujeet Singh Kapoor is a **1990 Batch IPS officer** and has previously been the **DGP of Haryana**.

**ITBP (Indo-Tibetan Border Police)**
- **Role:** Security of the **India–China Border**.
- **Established:** 1962.
- **Headquarter:** New Delhi.
- **Under:** Ministry of Home Affairs.

question: Where will the 9th International Spice Conference (ISC 2026) be held?
detail:
- The **9th International Spice Conference (ISC 2026)** will be organized from 23 to 26 February 2026 in **Kochi, Kerala**.
- This Conference will be organized by the **All India Spices Exporters Forum (AISEF)**.
- This conference will be inaugurated by **Amitabh Kant** (Former G20 Sherpa and Former CEO of NITI Aayog).

**International Spice Conference (ISC)**
- ISC is a **Global Platform** where Government Officials, Spice Exporters, Scientists & Researchers, and Policy Makers come together to discuss Spice Industry Growth, Innovation, and Supply Chain.

**Note**
- India is the world's largest **Spice Producer, Consumer, and Exporter**, often called the **"Land of Spices"**.
- Kerala is called the **'Spice Garden'** and **'Spice Capital of the World'**.

question: ECINET Digital Platform has been launched by which institution?
detail:
- The **Election Commission of India (ECI)** launched the **ECINET Platform** at the **International Conference on Democracy & Election Management (IICDEM) 2026**.
- Election Management (IICDEM) 2026 was organized in **New Delhi** on 21–23 January 2026.
- It was organized under the leadership of Chief Election Commissioner **Gyanesh Kumar**.

**What is ECINET?**
- ECINET is an **Integrated Digital System** of the Election Commission.
- In which all services and processes related to elections will be available on a single **Digital Platform**.
- It is being considered the world's largest **Electoral Digital Platform**.
- This Platform connects **40 Apps and Web Services** into a single Integrated System.
- The Platform is available in English and all **22 Indian Languages**.
- ECINET will increase **Transparency and Efficiency** in the Election Process, provide Real-time Information on Elections, and strengthen Citizens' Participation.

question: Sir Mark Tully has passed away recently, he is related to which field?
detail:
- **Sir Mark Tully** passed away at the age of 90.
- Sir Mark Tully was a **Former journalist** of **BBC** and was known as the **“Voice of India”**.

**He covered many historical moments of India**
- 1975 Emergency
- Operation Blue Star (1984)
- Indira Gandhi & Rajiv Gandhi assassinations
- 1991 Economic Liberalisation
- Babri Masjid demolition (1992)
- **Full Name:** Sir William Mark Tully.
- **Born:** 1935, Calcutta (now Kolkata).
- **Nationality:** British.

question: Actress Sharada has been honored with the J.C. Daniel Award for the year 2024, this award is given by which state government?
detail:
- Veteran Actress **Sharada** has been selected for the **J.C. Daniel Award** for the year 2024.
- This Award is the highest honor given by the **Kerala Government** for **Lifetime Contribution** to **Malayalam Cinema**.
- Sharada has become the **32nd film personality** to receive this honor.
- This veteran actress has won **National Awards** three times.

**J.C. Daniel Award**
- **Start:** 1992.
- **Prize Money:** ₹5 Lakh.
- This award is given by the Kerala Government.

**Objective**
- To honor **Lifetime Achievement** in Malayalam Cinema.
- It is considered the **Highest Civilian Honour** of Malayalam Cinema.
- This award is named after **J. C. Daniel**, the father of Malayalam Cinema.

question: In which state is the origin of the Tamsa river?
detail:
- **Narendra Modi** mentioned the **Renovation** of the **Tamsa River** in his Monthly Radio Program **‘Mann Ki Baat’** (130th Episode).
- He said that the people of **Azamgarh** in **Uttar Pradesh** made the river **continuous (aviral)** again through **Collective effort**.

**Tamsa (Tons) River**
- **Origin:** Tamsa Kund, Maihar (Satna District), Kaimur Ranges, **Madhya Pradesh**.
- **Length:** 264–265 km.
- **Confluence with Ganga:** Sirsa (near Prayagraj) in Uttar Pradesh.

**Flowing states**
- Madhya Pradesh.
- Uttar Pradesh.

question: Which country's parliament has passed a bill banning social media for children under 15 years of age?
detail:
- **France's National Assembly** (Lower House) has passed a **Bill** banning the use of **Social Media Platforms** for children under **15 years** of age.

**Aim**
- Reduce **Screen addiction** in children.
- Protect children from **Mental health crisis**.
- Control **Algorithm-driven influence**.
- There is a plan to implement this law on new user accounts from the **2026 Academic Session**.

**What is Age Verification Mechanism?**
- Social Media companies will have to:
  - Verify the **real age** of Users.
  - **Deactivate** Ineligible accounts by the end of 2026.
  - Implementation will be made in accordance with **EU laws**.

**Note**
- **Australia** is the first country in the world to ban Social Media use for children under **16 years** of age.

question: The Men's Hockey India League 2025-26 title was won by defeating which team in the final?
detail:
- **Kalinga Lancers** won the **Men’s Hockey India League 2026** title by defeating **Ranchi Royals** 3–2 in the Final played in **Bhubaneswar**.
- This is Kalinga Lancers' **second HIL title**; previously they won the trophy in **2017**.

**Hockey India League (HIL)**
- Hockey India League (HIL) is India's top **Professional Hockey League**.
- It is organized by **Hockey India**.

**Men’s Hockey India League 2025-26**
- **Final Venue:** Bhubaneswar (Kalinga Hockey Stadium).
- **Winner:** Kalinga Lancers.
- **Runners-up:** Ranchi Royals.

**Men’s Hockey India League (HIL) History**
- **First Season:** 2013.
- **Winner:** Ranchi Rhinos.
- **Runners-up:** Delhi Waveriders.

question: PM SVANidhi Credit Card was launched by Prime Minister Narendra Modi from which state?
detail:
- **Prime Minister Narendra Modi** launched the **PM Svanidhi Credit Card** from **Thiruvananthapuram (Kerala)**.
- With this Card, **Small Business Owners** will be connected to the **Formal Banking System**.
- This card was given to about **10,000 Street Vendors** in Kerala.
- It is a **UPI-Linked Rupay Credit Card**.
- The initial **Operating Limit** is **₹10,000**, which can increase up to **₹30,000** if usage is good.
- The **Validity** of the Card is **5 years** and it will offer **20–50 days of Interest-free Credit**.
- Only those Vendors who have fully repaid the first two loans of **PM SVANidhi** will get it.

**Objective**
- To provide **On-demand Credit** for Small Businesses.

**Note**
- The Union Cabinet has decided to extend the PM SVANidhi Scheme from December 31, 2024, to **March 31, 2030**.
- Along with this, the **Loan Amount** given in this Scheme has also been increased.

**PM SVANidhi Scheme**
- **Launch:** June 01, 2020.
- **Ministry:** Ministry Of Housing And Urban Affairs.
- **Objective:** This Scheme was brought to give **Affordable Loan** to **Street Vendors** during the time of Covid-19 so that they can use it to restart their work.

**How Much Loan Is Given Under This Scheme?**
- Under this, a Street Vendor can take Loan **3 times**: 1. **15,000** (Previously 10,000). 2. **25,000** (Previously 20,000). 3. **50,000** (No Changes).

question: The Free Trade Agreement between India and the European Union has been signed, in which year was the European Union established?
detail:
- Prime Minister Narendra Modi co-chaired the **16th India-EU Summit** in New Delhi on **27th January 2026** with European Council President **Antonio Costa** and European Commission President **Ursula von der Leyen**.
- A **Free Trade Agreement** was signed between India and the European Union.
- India-EU talks on Free Trade Agreement started in **2007**.
- **Current Bilateral Trade:** 136 billion US Dollars.

**Why Is This FTA Necessary For India?**
- India will get a new **Market** for Export.
- EU will make **Long Term Investment** in India.
- Export of Indian **MSME** will increase.

**European Union**
- It is a union of **27 countries** of Europe.
- EU has about **5.5%** of the world's Population but it contributes about **14 to 17%** to the world's GDP.
- **Formation: 1993** (Created by **Treaty Of Maastricht**).
- **Largest country of EU:** Germany.
- **Newest joining country:** Croatia (1 July 2013).
- **Note:** In 2020, **UK** left the European Union, after which it had 27 Members.

question: Saranda Forest was in discussion due to ‘Operation Megaburu’, in which state is it located?
detail:
- **17 Maoists** have been killed by security forces during **‘Operation Megaburu’** carried out in **Saranda Forest (West Singhbhum)** of **Jharkhand**.
- Out of the killed Maoists, a total reward of **₹4.49 crore** was declared on 13.
- This campaign was jointly carried out by **CRPF's CoBRA Units** and **Jharkhand Police**.

**What is "Operation Megaburu"?**
- Operation Megaburu is a major **Counter- terrorism operation** carried out against **CPI (Maoist)** in the Saranda Forest area of Jharkhand.

question: Which country has officially pulled out of the Paris Climate Agreement for the second time?
detail:
- The **United States of America** announced its official withdrawal from the **Paris Climate Agreement** for the **second time**.
- This decision has been taken by **US President Donald Trump**.
- America has now joined the few countries like **Iran, Libya, and Yemen** that are out of the Paris Agreement.

**Global Impact Analysis**
- America is the world's **Second Largest CO2 Emitter**, so its withdrawal weakens **Global Climate Governance**.
- However, **Renewable Energy Investment** is still growing rapidly globally.
- China is playing a key role in **Clean Energy Transition**.

**Paris Climate Agreement**
- **Adopted:** 2015, COP21 (Paris).
- **Implemented:** 2016.
- **Nature:** Legally Binding International Treaty.
- **Members:** 195 (194 Countries + EU).

question: The state of Kerala has declared which microbe as its state microbe?
detail:
- **Kerala** Officially declared **Bacillus subtilis** as its **State Microbe**.
- This announcement was made by **Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan** in Thiruvananthapuram.
- Kerala has become the **first state in India** to do so.
- The objective of this initiative is to promote **Science-based Governance**, **Research**, and **Knowledge Economy**.

**Bacillus subtilis**
- Bacillus subtilis is a beneficial **Probiotic bacteria**, naturally found in soil, fermented foods, and the human gut.
- It is considered helpful in improving **Intestine health**, boosting **Immunity**, improving soil fertility, and controlling plant diseases.

question: The UNESCO World Heritage Centre has included the Buddhist Diamond Triangle in the tentative list of UNESCO, which place is not included in this triangle?
detail:
- UNESCO World Heritage Centre has included **Odisha's Buddhist Diamond Triangle** in **India's Tentative List**.
- **Buddhist Diamond Triangle** - includes **Lalitgiri, Udayagiri, and Ratnagiri**. (Sanchi is in Madhya Pradesh, hence not included).
- This triangle is located in **Jajpur and Cuttack districts** of Odisha.
- It is situated in the middle of the **Asia mountain range** of Odisha.
- It is believed that all three sects of the Buddhist Religion - **Hinayana, Mahayana, and Vajrayana** - spread at these three Buddhist sites.

**UNESCO World Heritage List**
- This is a place that is recognized at the **Global Level** by UNESCO.
- These Sites are of 3 types - **Cultural, Natural, and Mixed**.
- **Formation:** 1978.

**United Nations Educational, Scientific And Cultural Organization (UNESCO)**
- It is a **Special Agency** of the UN.
- **Work:** It works in the field of **Education, Arts, Sciences, and Culture**.
- **Formation:** 1945.
- **Headquarters:** Paris, France.
- **Director-general:** Khaled El-enany.

question: Which of the following has become the first Indian shuttler to win 500 singles matches?
detail:
- **P. V. Sindhu** has become the **First Indian shuttler** to win **500 Singles Matches**.
- She achieved this achievement in **Indonesia Masters Super 500**.
- P. V. Sindhu is the world's **6th Female Player** who has won 500 Singles Matches.

**PV Sindhu**
- Belongs to **Telangana**.
- An **Indian Badminton Player**.
- She is the first and only Indian player to become a **Badminton World Champion**.

**Awards**
- Arjuna Award (2013)
- Padma Shri (2015)
- Major Dhyan Chand Khel Ratna (2016)
- Padma Bhushan (2020)
